mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings and improves economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant after adjusting for macro volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts signal strong household balance sheets and credit availability, which rapidly translates into positive equity market sentiment over the following week.
failure_condition: Housing start spikes are followed by negative 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when mortgage rates exceed 5.0%.
